ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-07-2018 Continued To Aug-21-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Jewish Family & Children's Services.

The Court expressed concern stating that it is not sure why it has taken as long as it has to distribute the reserve and why it required an additional petition when a final distribution had already been granted, and that this could have been by way of an ex parte petition. The Court also raised the issue of delay and extra work that went into this, expressed concern about the request for Trustee's fees when the Trustee had requested guideline fees in the last accounting indicating that it was reasonable and that the additional work would be done, and stated that there are a lot of Attorney's fees. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she received the Probate Examiner's notes on Friday, that there were several mistakes, that she would have liked to file something in response to the notes, that the delay had to do with the Japanese Beneficiaries, that Japan requires a lot of tax documents and that the Estate could not be closed. Ms. Friedeberg also stated that the Trustee's fees was a mistake, that she should not have requested them, informed the Court that the Trustee has exceeded the 1% guideline and went over by several thousands, stated that she will file a supplement for those amounts, that the Attorney time expended was expended, and that it was her understanding when the reserve is high, the Court wanted a full accounting. The First Supplemental Account and Report to Second and Final Account, etc. is continued to August 21, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to file a supplement.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (855 La Playa Street, #154, San Francisco, Ca 94121)
Off Calendar Feb-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Jewish Family & Children's Services.

The Court asked if a reappraisal for sale had been filed. Ms. Friedeberg stated that the initial inventory was filed in the conservatorship, that this was the first inventory filed in the trust, that they checked the codes (10309(1) & 2543(c)) and that there is no indication that it must be marked reappraisal for sale, that it would not have made sense as this is the first inventory & appraisal in the trust and provided a copy of the inventory & appraisal. The Court stated that the earlier appraisal is in the conservatorship case, that it will go ahead with the sale today subject to review. The sale for 100% interest was confirmed at $500,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed 1/14/16. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $600,000.00 is required.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing 4th And Final Accounting
Off Calendar Dec-02-2016 Off Calendar

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (855 La Playa Street, #162, Sf Ca 94121)
Off Calendar Dec-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg, for petitioner Jewish Family & Children's Services

Ms. Friedeberg stated that the petition was for sale of a 100% interest in the real property located at 855 La Playa #162, San Francisco, California, under the terms and conditions of the Report of Sale filed on November 20, 2015, with a purchase bid of $550,000. She stated that the first overbid would be in the amount of $578,000, with the second overbid of $583,000. The Court asked if there were any overbidders. An overbid was made by Mayukh Majumbar and Ratna Raha in the amount of $578,000. There were no further bids. The Court stated that it would confirm the sale in the amount of $578,000 upon the terms and conditions of the report of sale. The bidders inquired if it would be acceptable to the seller for a modification of the terms to permit closing within 30 days. Ms. Friedeberg confirmed that a 30-day closing was acceptable to the seller. The sale was confirmed at $570,000 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale, with the closing of escrow extended to 30 days. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $557,000 is required.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not reported)

Ex Parte Petition To Shorten The Time Of Notice Of Sale
Off Calendar Dec-16-2015 Off Calendar

Off Calendar Dec-14-2015 Continued To Dec-21-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for (trustee) Jewish Family & Children's Services.

The Court stated that it will need to continue the hearing for publishing. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she thought they had published and that the reappraisal was filed late last week. The Court stated that it was inadequate and that it could shorten time to 5 days. The report of sale and petition to confirm sale of real property was continued to December 21, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-03-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PCN-13-297115 Conservatorship of Nancy Tokuko Cook and PTR-13-297183 In re: Nancy Tokuko Cook.
Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for (conservator/trustee) Jewish Family & Children's Services.

The Court asked if the new Wells Fargo stock would be in the next accounting. Ms. Friedeberg confirmed that it would, that they will need to file a final accounting, apologized for the late filings and presented the court with a file endorsed copy of a declaration. The accounting petitions (both cases) were taken under submission. A status on filing the 4th and final accounting in the trust matter was set for January 4, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For Authority To Sell Real Property Without Publication, And For Authority To Grant Exclusive Right To Sell Real Property
Off Calendar Aug-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Jewish Family & Children's Services; Michael O'Connor, case manager for Jewish Family & Children's Services
The Court inquired why it was appropriate to waive publication regarding the sale. Ms.Friedeberg responded that Petitioner did not have to have publication waived, but that doind so would save time. The Court inquired how much time would be needed if the hearing were continued to permit publication. Ms. Friedeberg replied three weeks. The hearing on the petition was continued to August 24, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, to provide time for completion of publication, at which time the Court may deem the matter submitted. Upon further consideration, the Court granted authority to sell the properties and to enter into the listing agreements, but with publication of notice of sale (subject to review of order).
Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)
